human_verification.notes: Reviewed A/E resource-planning outputs for boundary discipline, design-phase specificity, licensure handling, subconsultant and BIM dependency awareness, QA/QC gating, and approval-refusal behavior.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicitly limited to recommendation-only A/E resource planning, phase and deliverable framing, discipline-capacity analysis, licensure-aware staffing checks, and escalation packaging.
Authoritative sources cover A/E industry boundaries, public-owner delivery workflow, schedule and cost management, BIM execution planning, qualifications artifacts, and national licensure baselines.
Research scenario families confirm evidence gaps, anti-generic handling, adjacent-lane routing, capacity shortfall, tie-break conflict, final-approval refusal, and tenant adaptation.

Evaluation scenario set covers realistic A/E phases and artifacts, capacity overload, conflicting priorities, licensure and QA/QC tradeoffs, adjacent-specialist rejection, low-confidence escalation, and refusal to treat the industry name as cosmetic.
Manifest and deployment notes keep runtime strategy as specification-based with explicit promotion criteria for retained implementation.

review_notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguities were left in the research contract coverage.
Boundary handling remained strong across licensure, fee authority, contract limits, BIM and QA/QC readiness, and final-approval refusal cases.

notes: This pack is designed for validated deployment as a spec-pack and does not claim retained implementation authority.
Promotion to a retained implementation must be explicit and separate.
Functionality coverage is complete in both the evaluation results and the functional audit record.
